title: Accessible Content Models, Landmarks, Headings, Links, Media, and Language · Spiral Review
summary: A delayed publication review reconstructs accessible content models, landmarks, headings, links, media, and language from revision, configuration, source bytes, document nodes, route manifest, rendered HTML, asset graph, validation report, artifact digest, deployment, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for WCAG 2.2 content and template conformance gate after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, cau…
objectives: Enforce document language, unique main, named navigation, logical heading outline, bypass mechanism, title, and meaningful sequence.
Require meaningful link text, current-location evidence, multiple ways to find pages, breadcrumb semantics, and non-color state.
Validate image purpose, alt decision, captions, transcripts, audio description needs, decorative handling, and accessible fallback.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Landmark Heading Model · changed case · Review Landmark Heading M…
steps.instruction:
review case — A review publishing team handles case 6400 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate generated page structure and content on WCAG evidence and human reader tasks; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

Competency probe: Combine structural checks, keyboard and zoom review, screen-reader tasks, content review, changed routes, and human ownership.

For Static-site publishing and Python Separate evidence from confidence · Review Accessibility Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: An automated accessibility scanner can certify the whole static site.

stimulus.title: Accessibility Evidence case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review publishing team handles case 3546 during an offline-first field documentation build. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate generated page structure and content on WCAG evidence and human reader tasks; preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions; then replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Combine structural checks, keyboard and zoom review, screen-reader tasks, content review, changed routes, and human ownership.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: An automated accessibility scanner can certify the whole static site.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Combine structural checks, keyboard and zoom review, screen-reader tasks, content review, changed routes, and human ownership. Artifact under review: WCAG 2.2 content and template conformance gate.

options.text: For separate evidence from confidence, render one trusted short page at the root, omit content authority, source spans, parser limits, route base, URL and asset reconciliation, contextual encoding, accessibility, clean-build comparison, deployment, rollback, recovery, and production transfer, then accept plausible HTML as publication proof. This does not satisfy Accessibility Evidence for WCAG 2.2 content and template conformance gate.
The record names the assumption, applies Accessibility Evidence, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Accessibility Evidence record says WCAG 2.2 content and template conformance gate looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Accessibility Evidence.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Record what required effort · Review Accessibility Evidence
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Accessibility Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review publishing team handles case 5165 during an accessibility-led community newsroom release. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate generated page structure and content on WCAG evidence and human reader tasks; preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Combine structural checks, keyboard and zoom review, screen-reader tasks, content review, changed routes, and human ownership.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Record what required effort · Review Accessibility Evidence.
steps.why: Retrieving Accessibility Evidence through the concrete WCAG 2.2 content and template conformance gate case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Record what required effort · Review Accessibility Evidence, keep Accessibility Evidence connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Combine structural checks, keyboard and zoom review, screen-reader tasks, content review, changed routes, and human ownership.
